2606.12200.title: Implicit Neural Representations of Individual Behavior
2606.12200.paper_summary: We study policy representation learning from unlabeled multi-policy behavioral data. Each episode is generated by a fixed policy, but policy labels are unavailable. This setting appears in robotics play, demonstrations, games, racing, and other datasets where heterogeneous behaviors are mixed without annotations. We introduce \emph{Behavioral INR}, a self-supervised generative model that adapts implicit neural representations (INRs) from vision to behavior. Instead of mapping coordinates to RGB values, Behavioral INR represents a policy as a state-action function mapping states to subsequent actions. An episode-level latent modulates this function through FiLM layers, yielding a generative prior over policies and allowing policy identity to be inferred without supervision. Because INRs treat each datapoint as samples from an underlying function, the same model naturally accommodates variable episode lengths and different sampling granularities, as in vision INRs with different image resolutions. We also define policy-level out-of-distribution (OOD) shifts along state-distribution and action-distribution axes, which arise when policies overlap in states or actions but are not captured by standard behavioral OOD settings based only on new agents or environments. We evaluate on synthetic Gaussian random field data, MuJoCo demonstrations with controlled OOD splits, and real-world chess, Formula 1 racing, robotics, and Seek-Avoid datasets. Behavioral INR most consistently improves policy identifiability in the hardest continuous state-action settings, especially when longer episodes, more policies, and OOD splits reduce the usefulness of marginal shortcuts; amortized history encoders remain competitive when policy identity can be recovered from symbolic repetition or low-dimensional action statistics. We release code and checkpoints.
2606.12200.paper_summary_zh: 我們研究從未標記的多策略行為數據中進行政策表示學習。每個回合是由固定策略生成的，但策略標籤是不可用的。這種設置出現在機器人遊戲、示範、遊戲、賽車以及其他異質行為混合且未註釋的數據集中。我們介紹了 \emph{Behavioral INR}，這是一種自我監督的生成模型，將隱式神經表示（INRs）從視覺適應到行為。Behavioral INR 不是將坐標映射到 RGB 值，而是將策略表示為一個狀態-行動函數，將狀態映射到隨後的行動。回合級潛在變量通過 FiLM 層調節此函數，產生對策略的生成先驗，並允許在沒有監督的情況下推斷策略身份。因為 INRs 將每個數據點視為來自潛在函數的樣本，所以同一模型自然適應變化的回合長度和不同的採樣粒度，正如在具有不同圖像解析度的視覺 INRs 中所見。我們還定義了沿著狀態分佈和行動分佈軸的政策級別分佈外（OOD）變化，這些變化出現在策略在狀態或行動上重疊但未被僅基於新代理或環境的標準行為 OOD 設置捕捉時。我們在合成高斯隨機場數據、具有控制 OOD 拆分的 MuJoCo 示範以及真實世界的棋類、F1 賽車、機器人技術和 Seek-Avoid 數據集上進行評估。Behavioral INR 在最困難的連續狀態-行動設置中最一致地提高了策略可識別性，特別是在較長的回合、更多策略和 OOD 拆分降低了邊際捷徑的有效性時；當策略身份可以從符號重複或低維行動統計中恢復時，攤銷歷史編碼器仍然具有競爭力。我們釋放了代碼和檢查點。
2606.12200.author: Andrew Kang et.al.
